UK version-spike evidence — Data Protection Act 2018 (ukpga/2018/12)
=====================================================================

Goal: prove that we can extract at least 2 distinct versions with effective
dates for a single UK Act, per Step 0.5 of ADDING_A_COUNTRY.md.

Source: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/

Access pattern for point-in-time versions:
    /{type}/{year}/{number}/{version}/data.xml
  where {version} is one of:
    - enacted                 → the as-Royal-Assent immutable text
    - YYYY-MM-DD              → the consolidated text as at that date
    - (omitted)               → the latest revised text

For DPA 2018, we fetched three distinct versions:

  file                                 Effective date         Provisions  Schedules  Body chars
  -----------------------------------  ---------------------  ----------  ---------  ----------
  sample-dpa-2018-enacted.xml          2018-05-23 (enacted)         1104         21     615,373
  sample-dpa-2018-pit-2023.xml         2022-12-05 (RestrictStartDate)1116        22     600,125
  sample-dpa-2018-latest.xml           2026-04-07 (RestrictStartDate)1182        24     596,955

Observations:
  - The enacted root has no RestrictStartDate — that is how the pipeline can
    distinguish "as-enacted" from a point-in-time snapshot.
  - The revised versions carry RestrictStartDate on the root plus
    Match/RestrictStartDate/RestrictEndDate attributes on every modified
    provision. We do not need to re-evaluate these ourselves; the server
    returns pre-filtered content for the requested date.
  - Provision count grows with amendments (new sections inserted), confirming
    that text content legitimately differs between versions.

Discovery of effective dates for commit timeline:

  /changes/affected/{type}/{year}/{number}/data.feed?results-count=N&page=M
  (Atom feed — each entry carries a <ukm:Effect> with <ukm:InForceDates>)

  For DPA 2018 the first-page of that feed alone (50 entries out of 2,270)
  already surfaces 3 distinct Applied="true" in-force dates:

    2025-06-19
    2026-03-31
    2026-04-07

  To bootstrap the full commit history of an Act, the fetcher will:
    1. Fetch /{type}/{year}/{number}/enacted/data.xml     → commit[0]
    2. Page through the changes feed, collecting every
       (Applied=true, Date) pair into a sorted-unique list of dates.
    3. For each date D in that list, fetch
       /{type}/{year}/{number}/{D}/data.xml               → commit[i]
       with Source-Id = AffectingURI extracted from the <ukm:Effect> group.

Conclusion: version access is rich and well-structured. GATE 0.5 PASSES.
